name: google
tagline: Small changes, fast presubmits, nothing lands that lowers code health.
philosophy: Code review exists to keep the codebase healthy over decades. A change must improve overall code health, even if it is not perfect.

gates.description: If you liked the behavior, you shoulda put a test on it: any behavior change without a covering test blocks GREEN.
Diff beyond ~400 changed lines: advise splitting into smaller increments; note it in the review.
The reviewer verdict must state that the change improves or maintains overall code health.
A test that passes only on retry is reported as FLAKY and quarantined in the report — never silently retried to green, never 'fixed' by the builder.
